message:
test: repair 28 more pre-existing failures across 4 crates

Sweep of crates I hadn't yet tested found 28 broken tests. All trace to
production behavior changes that landed without updating the
corresponding test fixtures or expectations. No production behavior is
changed in this commit (one carefully-bounded production fix in
fcp-registry; see below).

fcp-manifest (10 failures → 0):
  Commit d8dd6bb5a (2026-04-19) added a manifest validation rule:
  every operation's `capability` MUST appear in `capabilities.required`
  or `capabilities.optional`. The base test fixture's
  `provides.operations.telegram_send_message.capability` was
  `telegram.send_message`, but `capabilities.required` did not list it.
  Three tests that string-replaced the required/forbidden lists also
  needed to know the new fixture shape. One additional test
  (`vector_manifest_minimal_parses`) was orphaned by commit 9c3a290e
  which updated the test vector to match the same rule but left the
  assertion at `len() == 1`.

fcp-raptorq (4 failures → 0):
  - `decoder_direct_add_symbol_enforces_symbol_slot_cap`: used K=1
    OTI but K=1 has K_padded=10 (RFC 6330 K' table), so once
    received_count >= K, every subsequent add_symbol triggered
    `try_reconstruct` whose pre-flight check rejects ESIs in
    [K..K_padded). Switched to K=10 (smallest K with K_padded == K),
    adjusted slot count to the resulting `1010` budget.
  - `decoder_add_symbol_duplicate_no_count_increase` and
    `decode_with_context_duplicate_symbols_do_not_consume_permit_budget`:
    used DIFFERENT payloads for the same ESI to test "duplicate
    ignored" semantics. Production now (correctly) rejects payload-
    conflicting duplicates as `InvalidSymbol` — that's a separate
    error class covered by `decoder_rejects_conflicting_duplicate_*`.
    Switched to true (identical-payload) duplicates so the original
    invariant under test fires.
  - `decode_with_context_cancellation_after_acquire_releases_admission_slot`:
    `validate_decode_bounds()` (added in 9ce1cacc8 on 2026-04-20) now
    refuses any decode whose declared `transfer_length` exceeds
    `max_object_size`. The test's 3.2 MB transfer with the default
    test_config 1 MiB cap short-circuited BEFORE acquiring an
    admission slot, so the rendezvous on `active_count == 1` never
    fired. Test now uses a per-test config with a 4 MiB cap.

fcp-registry (6 failures → 0):
  - 5 tests broke on the same d8dd6bb5a manifest rule above; fixed by
    adding `test.op` to the test fixture's `capabilities.required`.
  - `metamorphic_install_verify_reinstall_round_trip_is_observationally_idempotent`
    asserts that re-running install/verify/mirror on the same bundle
    produces the same observable state. Object IDs are blake3-256
    over `(header, body)`, so an `ObjectStoreError::AlreadyExists` for
    a derived ID is a guarantee of byte-identical content. The
    previous mirror_bundle code propagated `AlreadyExists` as an error,
    which broke the metamorphic invariant on any re-mirror. Production
    fix (small): treat `AlreadyExists` as success in `mirror_bundle`'s
    two `store.put(...)` calls, with an explicit comment naming the
    blake3-collision-resistance assumption that makes this safe. Other
    `ObjectStoreError` variants still propagate. All 4 sibling
    `mirror_bundle_*` tests still pass.

fcp-tailscale (7 failures → 0):
  - 5 `fcp_tags_*` tests built `MeshIdentity::new(...)` without a
    `with_attestation(...)` call. Commit 8a0d49596 (2026-04-20)
    tightened `fcp_tags()` to require a valid attestation, so
    pre-fix it returned the empty Vec and assertions on count failed.
    Each test now signs the (node_id, node_keys, tags) triple with
    the owner key and chains `.with_attestation(attestation)`.
  - `test_mesh_identity_many_tags`: same attestation gating.
  - `test_node_id_serde_roundtrip_unicode`: was BORN broken — the test
    asserts that a Unicode `NodeId` round-trips through serde, but
    `TailscaleNodeId::try_new` (the deserialize path) has rejected
    non-ASCII since fcp-core 19745bc08 (2026-01-15), well before this
    test was written (2026-03-07). The constructor `NodeId::new` is
    permissive but deserialize is strict by design — that's a security
    invariant (no non-canonical node identities can be smuggled via
    wire payloads). Repurposed the test to pin the actual contract:
    serialization of a permissively-constructed Unicode NodeId
    succeeds, but deserialization rejects it with the documented
    "must be ASCII" error.

Verified: all four crates are now green:
  cargo test -p fcp-manifest --lib  →  562/562
  cargo test -p fcp-raptorq  --lib  →  762/762
  cargo test -p fcp-registry --lib  →  362/362
  cargo test -p fcp-tailscale --lib →  482/482
